preview.canonicalTitle: cross-viewport UI reliability and layout hardening
preview.canonicalBody:
<!-- ATLAS-CARD:START -->
ATLAS-CARD-ID: `mazer-cross-viewport-ui-reliability`
- project: `Mazer`
- type: `feature`
- state: `review`
- priority: `High`
- owner: `Mazer`
- progress: `90%`
- autonomous implementation: `not_admitted`
- updated: `2026-07-15T19:31:32.106Z`

## Summary
Coordinate mobile safe-area, shared overlay geometry, and browser layout through one no-overlap contract and proof matrix.

## Objective
- Make menu, Options, play, and Pause remain readable and operable across phone, browser, PWA, and restored-window surfaces without per-screenshot offsets.

## Acceptance criteria
- Every primary Mazer surface has an owner, supported viewport category, and no-overlap contract.
- Mobile and browser layout work can be verified independently and together through a shared matrix.
- No completion claim relies on desktop-only proof for a phone or restored-window defect.
- Physical-device findings remain open until operator screenshots confirm the deployed behavior.

## Discoveries
- Operator production screenshots correctly rejected the earlier review evidence because status, guide, and action text still visibly touched or crossed chrome.
- Logical Phaser bounds under-reported physical painted text width, so the proof contract now requires stronger insets and excludes text from active scroll-fade bands.

## Next actions
- Review commit c7b1540e on a physical iPhone using the exact corrected preview and attach menu, Options, play, and Pause screenshots.
- Keep native browser chrome and maximize or restore proof open until physical evidence passes.

## Blockers
- None

## Evidence
- Mazer draft PR #76 at head c7b1540e.
- Focused visible-text verification passed 62 tests; full verification passed 363 tests and the production build.
- Preview dpl_FGKmMPHb64mGqdGykzQAmZ6YspNX passed exact deployed 393x852 containment, fade-clearance, overlap, and console checks.
<!-- ATLAS-CARD:END -->
preview.journalBody:
ATLAS-JOURNAL-EVENT-ID: `mazer-cross-viewport-ui-reliability-pr76-visible-text-correction-20260715`
- card: `mazer-cross-viewport-ui-reliability`
- idempotency: `mazer-cross-viewport-ui-reliability-pr76-visible-text-correction-20260715`
## Operator-visible text bleed corrected on the exact PR 76 preview
- kind: `review`
- state: `review`
- actor: `Mazer`
- occurred: `2026-07-15T19:31:32.106Z`

## Completed
- Added physical safety insets to shared run-status, guide, toggle, and action text.
- Prevented partial guide or row text from rendering beneath active scroll fades.
- Re-proved the exact deployed 393x852 menu, Options, play, and Pause surfaces.

## Discovered
- The previous automated pass was insufficient because it measured logical bounds more optimistically than the phone painted glyphs.

## Next
- Collect operator iPhone screenshots from the corrected preview before production promotion or completion.

## Blockers
- None

## Evidence
- https://github.com/fawxzzy/mazer/pull/76
- Mazer head c7b1540e
- Vercel deployment dpl_FGKmMPHb64mGqdGykzQAmZ6YspNX

- branch: `codex/physical-ui-controls-rework-20260715`

preview.canonicalTitle: normalize browser UI layout across viewports
preview.canonicalBody:
<!-- ATLAS-CARD:START -->
ATLAS-CARD-ID: `mazer-browser-layout-persistence`
- project: `Mazer`
- type: `feature`
- state: `review`
- priority: `High`
- owner: `Mazer`
- progress: `89%`
- autonomous implementation: `not_admitted`
- updated: `2026-07-15T19:31:33.106Z`

## Summary
Normalize responsive browser geometry and preserve only semantic settings so viewport changes cannot replay stale layout.

## Objective
- Recompute all menu, Options, play, and Pause geometry from the active content rectangle while preserving explicit player preferences only.

## Acceptance criteria
- Layout-derived geometry is never persisted as player preference state.
- A recalculated layout cannot overlap, escape the viewport, or hide essential controls.
- Normal reload preserves supported semantic settings with safe migration defaults.
- Narrow, wide, phone, and restore endpoints have deterministic evidence.

## Discoveries
- A logical bounds pass did not guarantee that antialiased physical glyphs stayed visually clear of the panel rails.
- Scroll fades must reserve an explicit no-text render viewport rather than merely overlaying alpha on clipped content.

## Next actions
- Complete physical iPhone review of the corrected c7b1540e preview.
- Retain native browser chrome and maximize or restore as separate remaining evidence.

## Blockers
- None

## Evidence
- Mazer draft PR #76 at c7b1540e.
- Exact 393x852 deployed preview proof passed with no text under scroll fades and no console or page errors.
<!-- ATLAS-CARD:END -->
preview.journalBody:
ATLAS-JOURNAL-EVENT-ID: `mazer-browser-layout-persistence-pr76-visible-text-correction-20260715`

## Physical text containment and scroll-fade clearance strengthened
- kind: `review`
- state: `review`
- actor: `Mazer`
- occurred: `2026-07-15T19:31:33.106Z`

## Completed
- Reserved fade-safe scroll render bands for guide and settings content.
- Applied fitted typography to every overlay and menu action button.
- Added deployed checks for action-label insets and scroll-fade text clearance.

## Discovered
- Production screenshots remained on the older release while the corrected candidate was still in preview, which made the edit appear absent.

## Next
- Use operator physical screenshots to decide whether the corrected candidate may advance to production.

## Blockers
- None

## Evidence
- https://github.com/fawxzzy/mazer/pull/76
- Mazer head c7b1540e

preview.canonicalTitle: shared menu and play run status panel
preview.canonicalBody:
<!-- ATLAS-CARD:START -->
ATLAS-CARD-ID: `mazer-shared-run-status-panel`
- project: `Mazer`
- type: `feature`
- state: `review`
- priority: `High`
- owner: `Mazer`
- progress: `30%`
- autonomous implementation: `not_admitted`
- updated: `2026-07-15T19:31:34.106Z`

## Summary
Use one responsive menu and play status component for time, Rank or AI Rank, Score out of 100, and Maze level from the correct context.

## Objective
- Give menu and active play the same compact status language while keeping score authority traceable to the canonical scorer.

## Acceptance criteria
- Menu and play consume the same status-panel formatting and layout contract.
- Panel text remains centered, contained, and readable at phone and desktop widths.
- Panel width, gap, and play Pause placement satisfy shared viewport diagnostics.
- Displayed score is traceable to canonical run-quality data rather than a presentation-only calculation.

## Discoveries
- The play Maze value visibly touched the right rail even though the previous logical fit calculation passed.
- Physical containment needs conservative horizontal padding and a lower glyph-width safety ratio.

## Next actions
- Review the corrected menu and play status rows on a physical iPhone.
- Identify and consume the finalized canonical 0 to 100 scorer field before completion.

## Blockers
- None

## Evidence
- Mazer draft PR #76 at head c7b1540e uses the corrected shared status geometry.
- Exact deployed 393x852 menu and play status proof now has explicit rail clearance.
- Canonical score authority remains explicitly non-terminal.
<!-- ATLAS-CARD:END -->
preview.journalBody:

- card: `mazer-shared-run-status-panel`

## Shared status text now keeps physical rail clearance
- kind: `review`
- state: `review`
- actor: `Mazer`
- occurred: `2026-07-15T19:31:34.106Z`

## Completed
- Increased compact status-panel width and padding while reducing fitted font size.
- Strengthened the diagnostic to require horizontal and vertical text insets.
- Re-proved menu and play status rows on the exact preview deployment.

## Discovered
- The earlier shallow fit check was not a reliable proxy for painted phone glyph width.

## Next
- Confirm presentation with operator iPhone screenshots, then continue the scorer-owned dependency separately.

## Blockers
- None

## Evidence
